title: Query and update DOM

canonicalExplanation.summary: DOM queries select existing nodes and updates change properties, attributes, classes, or text with semantics appropriate to the content.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: querySelector is null-checked before textContent updates trusted text without parsing HTML. Your goal is to implement a DOM update without unsafe HTML parsing or unnecessary tree replacement. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Scope, closures and modules; html project site; html document dom; Dependency boundaries. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: DOM queries select existing nodes and updates change properties, attributes, classes, or text with semantics appropriate to the content. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Queries can return null or live collections, innerHTML parses markup, and property and attribute values can differ. This draft therefore rejects: innerHTML and textContent are interchangeable for displaying user text.

includes: Select elements and update text, attributes, classes and properties with correct distinctions.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Use textContent for text; innerHTML parses markup and is unsafe for untrusted strings without robust sanitization.
